creative_brief: Hero image: warm, slightly overexposed kitchen scene — a mum unpacking a brown paper bag of groceries onto a timber bench, a child reaching for an egg carton. No logos visible on products. Below-fold: mosaic of real farm photography — Rachel Ward's Kempsey property, Collins Wild Caught boats, Bundarra Farm paddocks. Earthy palette, no stock photography.
headline: "I just assumed grass-fed meant something. It doesn't."
subhead: A Northern Beaches mum started reading labels. What she found changed how she feeds her family — and led her to a grocery store built by a farmer who lost everything and started over.
hero_copy: She thought she was doing the right thing. Picking the pack that said grass-fed. Paying extra for free-range. Then she learned supermarket beef travels an average of 2,400km and sits in cold storage for up to 21 days before it hits the shelf. The label said one thing. The supply chain said another. She wanted a store that couldn't lie to her — where she could trace every cut back to a real paddock. That store didn't exist yet. So we built it.

sections.heading: A farmer who rebuilt from ash
sections.body: Rachel Ward spent years building a regenerative farm in Kempsey, NSW. Then bushfire took it. She could have walked away. Instead, she rebuilt — every paddock, every practice — around regenerative agriculture. Animals raised on pasture. No hormones. No antibiotics. No feedlot finishing. 100% grass-fed beef, days old when it reaches you, not weeks. Rachel became a key partner and supplier to FarmThru because she believed one farm couldn't fix the system alone. But a network of farms, connected to families who actually care? That could change everything.

sections.heading: We built the grocery store that labels can't fake
sections.body: FarmThru is farm-direct and 100% sourced from regenerative farms. Bundarra Farm supplies our pork. Collins Wild Caught brings wild-caught Alaskan salmon from the South Coast of NSW. Paris Creek Farms in Meadows, SA delivers carbon-neutral bio-dynamic dairy. Farmer Brown supplies our eggs. Every product moves straight from their farm to our hub to your kitchen — days old, not weeks old. You order online. You collect from our Brookvale hub, Monday to Friday. No middlemen inflating the price. No cold chain stretching across the country. Just real food from real farms, priced for everyday families.

sections.heading: This isn't just a store — you can own a piece of it
sections.body: FarmThru is opening an equity crowdfunding campaign. That means you don't just shop with us — you can be a partner in building a food system that works differently. Vote for change with your wallet. Be part of a grocery store that shows you exactly where your food comes from. Join the waitlist with a fully refundable $5 deposit and we'll notify you the moment the campaign goes live. No commitment. No obligation. Just first access to own a piece of something worth building. Investing in early-stage businesses carries risk, including the potential loss of your investment. This is not financial advice.

sections.heading: No commitment, just better food when you want it
sections.body: There's no subscription. No lock-in. You order when you want and collect from our Brookvale hub Monday to Friday. Regenerative beef, pasture-raised chicken, wild-caught salmon, heritage pork, eggs, dairy — all from farms you can actually name. Start with a single order or join the crowdfunding waitlist. Either way, you'll know exactly what you're feeding your family.

sections.heading: Compliance
sections.body: FarmThru's equity crowdfunding campaign is conducted under the Australian crowd-sourced funding regime. Investing in early-stage businesses carries significant risk, including the potential loss of your entire investment. Past performance is not a reliable indicator of future results. No returns are projected or guaranteed. You should consider whether this investment is appropriate for your circumstances and read the offer document in full before making any investment decision. This is general information only and not financial advice.
